summary: A crisp audience recording of the 1993 Louisville run captures a rousing show with standout jams on Saint, Foolish Heart and Stella Blue.
review: The tape is praised as one of the better ’90s audience recordings – instruments are distinct, cymbals clear and the low end round. The performance itself is described as one of the strongest of 1993, highlighted by a heavy‑duty Foolish Heart, a flawless Saint, an emotional Stella Blue and a dance‑floor‑firing Good Lovin'.

ai_rating.rationale: Three reviews (one neutral/negative about access, two strongly positive) give an overall high impression of both performance and sound quality; weighted rating lands just above 4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as one of the better audience tapes from the ’90s – distinct instrument separation, clear cymbals, nice round low end and overall crispness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls through classic acoustic‑rock numbers (Cold Rain, Same Thing, Peggy-O) and solid ’72 European classics. The second set launches into heavy, trippy jams – Foolish Heart → Saint → Stella Blue – creating a muscular, space‑filled vibe that culminates in an ecstatic Good Lovin' dancefest.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for wild‑card guitar work, shredding jams on Saint and Foolish Heart, and a heartfelt solo that moves listeners.
band_member_comments.Bob: His fuzzy, psychedelic tone is upfront in the mix; his vocal feedback after “it seems like all this life was just a dream” adds spine‑tingling atmosphere.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is noted as settled into the sole keyboard role, providing solid support without Hornsby’s influence.
